title: _interpchannels.create() desyncs its int error code from the exception state under OOM — handle_channel_error asserts (Modules/_interpchannelsmodule.c:398/443)
description: The _interpchannels create path threads a hand-rolled int error code in parallel with PyErr; handle_channel_error asserts they agree (err==0 => !PyErr_Occurred() at L398; unhandled err<0 => PyErr_Occurred() at L443). Under OOM newchannelid() returns 0 with a MemoryError pending, or channel_create() returns -1 with no exception set, so one of the two asserts aborts.
notes: Two faces of one defect: minimal repro (import _interpchannels; create(1) under set_nomemory sweep) deterministically hits L398 `!PyErr_Occurred()` (newchannelid returns 0 with MemoryError pending); vehicle hits L443 `PyErr_Occurred()` (channel_create returns -1 without setting an exception). Root cause = int error code vs PyErr desync in channel_create/newchannelid under OOM. Fix at the producers: channel_create must set an exception on every <0 return; newchannelid must return <0 whenever it leaves one set. Debug-only asserts (NDEBUG strips them). Distinct from OOM-0014 (channelsmod__channel_id, :3487). Exception-state-under-OOM family. Minimal repro reproduces deterministically (L398 form). One of 3 deferred abort singletons triaged 2026-06-18.
